page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.2xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARMv8 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with single-thread execution and an arm64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 4 Gbps, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Memory bandwidth tests show read-only speeds of 774.56 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 181.03 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, alongside a large-block write speed of 69.45 GB/sec. CPU performance is more moderate, with a Passmark CPU score in the bottom 25% and a multi-core stress-ng score of 12243.2. In application-specific workloads, the instance achieves an average of 12.13 million operations per second on Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 15.79 GB/sec. Large language model inference speeds range from an average of 422.26 tokens per second for small model prompt processing to 14.13 tokens per second for medium model prompt processing.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the r7gd.2xlarge offers a high density of memory and local NVMe storage relative to its 8-core compute profile, making it a resource-dense option for memory-bound workloads. However, the tradeoff lies in its moderate CPU compute performance and lack of hardware-accelerated graphics or AVX instruction sets. This server is best suited for memory-intensive applications, local caching layers, relational and non-relational databases, and web serving workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth and fast local storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server instance powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of memory and a bundled 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results demonstrate top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score in the top 10% and a small-block cached read bandwidth of 774.56 GB/sec. Conversely, CPU compute capabilities are moderate, with a Passmark CPU score in the bottom 25%. This resource density makes the instance highly efficient for memory-bound tasks. The r7gd.2xlarge is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and web serving applications requiring fast local storage and high memory capacity.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server featuring 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 474 GB NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.2xlarge is an arm64 memory-optimized server with 8 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An arm64 memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARMv8 cores at 2.6 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory with top-tier bandwidth performance
474 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for databases, caching layers, and memory-intensive web serving
